title: Race of Life
subTitle: Showcasing of how avian predators and prey attack and evade each other, including horizontal speeding, turning and diving.

title: Animal Society
subTitle: Caniforms inhabit various habitats worldwide; divided into nine families, including all bears, wolves and foxes, their size and lifestyles vary considerably.

title: Animal Society
subTitle: Ancient as the dinosaurs themselves take a look at the family structure of crocodiles, turtles and snakes.

title: INWILD
subTitle: The big cats are a group of apex predators that rest firmly at the top of the food chain; lions, leopards and tigers all live uniquely with particular hunting prowess, advantages and pack hierarchy or solitary territories.

title: INWILD
subTitle: Explore the worlds of hooved animals from deer and rhinos to pigs and goats. A diverse collection of some of the most interesting animals in the world.

title: Wild About
subTitle: Twenty-seven species of turtles are on the very brink of becoming extinct; over 240 additional species being threatened or critically endangered.

title: Animals at Work
subTitle: Miss Kitty the pig starts work in the fields, pest control dog Tracker sniffs out termites in Florida and a group of otters go on a playful PR mission in the UK.

title: Animals at Work
subTitle: An elephant named Bubbles prepares for her big show.

title: Animals at Work
subTitle: Nine irresistible Labrador pups get the chance to star in a TV ad campaign, a pair of heritage oxen stick together in Nova Scotia in Canada, and `Superdog' and his pals perform a high flying routine at half time.

title: Second Chance Pets
subTitle: A dog named Luna is hit by a car and needs a brace to avoid amputation; Miley the bulldog needs her brace adjusted.

title: Second Chance Pets
subTitle: Checking on a goat that may be a good candidate for a brace; a dog named Zachery Lee needs a brace to get back on the job at its owner's salon.

title: Second Chance Pets
subTitle: A dog named Nya was in a serious car accident and needs a brace to fully heal; a dog named Ringo had an injury when he was adopted and after years of bearing weight, needs a brace to help strengthen his leg.

subTitle: Vet Johan helps a young elephant tangled in wire; teaching a newborn giraffe to drink.

title: Shamwari
subTitle: A buffalo is tested for tuberculosis; baby elephant needs rescue; ranger watches lions kill wildebeest; two leopards get a second chance at life when they're rescued from a zoo in Monaco.

title: Shamwari
subTitle: An angry rhino attacks the wildlife team; an orphaned baby elephant gets a playmate; a capture team ranger injured by wildebeest; check out a rare footage of two leopards leaping.

title: Shamwari
subTitle: A tranquilized lion wakes up early; the staff struggles to get the baby elephant to drink milk; the game capture team tracks a hippo; a torrential downpour wreaks havoc at the animal hospital.

title: Wild Animal Rescue
subTitle: Four Paws swings into action to relocate the 15 remaining animals in desperate need of aid from the "worst zoo in the world," Khan Younis Zoo.

subTitle: The team puts their lives on the line to raise orphaned rhinos and return them to the wild; a high-stakes rescue mission gives big cats a new home in Lionsrock sanctuary.

subTitle: It's a year later and things have changed; Marlice and Rudie have a new baby boy and he needs to be introduced to the animals.

subTitle: While looking for the three missing caracals, Kiewiet finds suspicious-looking human tracks; Rudie and Flo investigate and believe the tracks belong to a poacher; Rudie, Kiewiet and Flo do a stakeout from a watchtower and later apprehend a suspect.

title: Orphans of the Wild
subTitle: One of the wild dogs has a snakebite wound; Marlice and Rudie take her to their clinic; a caracal with a broken leg arrives at Naankuse, and they take her to a vet in Windhoek.

subTitle: Examines the lives of the fox, lynx and harpy eagle, three majestic and cunning predators living in the wild.

title: Killer Instincts
subTitle: Examines the lives of the ferocious barracuda, the playful yet aggressive leopard seal and the powerful bull shark.

subTitle: Female sea turtles; western long-beaked echidna; Sumatran rhinoceros.

subTitle: Some animals have the aerial advantage; they're the fearless fliers that have the battlefield covered, scoping out the situation with super senses and the right stuff.

title: Wild Iberia
subTitle: Life begins once again as the sun is heating all in the Iberian peninsula; as animals awaken with the flowers, the softness of the weather has everyone building and breeding.

title: Wild Iberia
subTitle: With the exception of the mountains and the north of Iberia, almost everywhere else is over 30 C and some places in the south are even reaching 50 C, bringing about a great thirst among the animals.

title: Wild Iberia
subTitle: Summer ended several days ago and Autumn is invading the whole of Iberia as cicadas and the grasshoppers have ceased to sing and the ants will soon be eating their corpses.

title: Wild Iberia
subTitle: Summer ended several days ago and Autumn is invading the whole of Iberia as cicadas and the grasshoppers have ceased to sing and the ants will soon be eating their corpses.

title: Wild Iberia
subTitle: The time of stillness and silence has come, but most animals aren't able to hibernate; many species have had to adapt to this continental way of life.

title: Adaptable Animals
subTitle: The clock is ticking as Janice has to cast, create, and fit a test brace from scratch; a smooth fox terrier's mysterious issue continues to be a challenge; a post-surgical goldendoodle finds out if his healing is on track.

title: Adaptable Animals
subTitle: After being hit by a truck, an Australian Shepherd comes to a crossroads about if he can walk with a brace on all fours again or have to have an amputation.

title: Adaptable Animals
subTitle: Janice casts a pug for a neck brace and a cat for an orthotic boot; an elderly Shih Tzu hopes to find a simple solution for a possible neurological problem.

title: Adaptable Animals
subTitle: Three-legged rescues from Mexico and Russia are cast and fitted for new prosthetic legs.

subTitle: Debbie takes delivery of a precious package as a box of abandoned kittens arrives at the clinic.

title: Inside Animal A&E
subTitle: Behind the scenes at the Blue Cross animal hospitals in London Victoria and Grimsby.

title: Super Senses
subTitle: Animals that live underground have developed keen senses to find their way around; wombats have excellent hearing to compensate for their very small eyes; the mole is almost completely blind, but uses its nose to get along.

title: Super Senses
subTitle: Animals use all of their senses to communicate, to attract partners, discourage predators, and deceive their prey; they send messages without needing words.

title: Super Senses
subTitle: The most spectacular senses help animals rule their environment; the champions of the animal world reveal their special weapons and abilities.

title: Animals That Changed History
subTitle: Nothing changed the world more than the process of domestication; once humans found they could shape the environment and control the foods they grew, they gave up a nomadic hunter/gatherer lifestyle and became farmers.

subTitle: The dangerous jobs of people who get close enough to photograph sharks, interact with the predators and even wrestle with them.

subTitle: Zamir's twins, Gangol and Ragin, cause mayhem on city streets.

subTitle: Drawn in by the constant supply of food, urban coyotes cannot resist the sights and smells of Anchorage; highly elusive, no-one knows how many coyotes live in this city.

subTitle: Dogs: greyhounds' speed, bloodhounds' noses and border collies' brains.

subTitle: One of Alaska's most secretive and efficient predators, Lynx are usually highly elusive; in Anchorage, houses are being built in the Lynx's territory, forcing these usually shy big cats, to find new ways to survive on the streets.

subTitle: Exploring the most intelligent of animals; it could be the socially complex chimpanzee, the erudite elephant, the fastidious bird or the inimitable octopus.

title: Wild Ones
subTitle: On the world's largest island thrives a breathtaking variety of animals, including the bizarre platypus, adorable koalas, bouncing kangaroos and dingoes that prowl.

title: Wild Ones
subTitle: From pelican to chattering parrots, kestrels to eagles, there are lots of our flying, feathered friends to meet in this episode.

title: Wild Ones
subTitle: Investigates which is the biggest of them all. In the modern world, the largest land animals are native to Africa and Asia, but the oceans conceal true giants.

subTitle: Jonathan explores Lake Champlain's wrecks, focusing on a horse ferry wreck in Burlington Bay.

subTitle: Jonathan Bird investigates the underwater world, finding manatees and renting scuba tanks.

subTitle: Marine naturalist and underwater filmmaker Jonathan Bird discusses animal themes, marine research, underwater exploration and recent discoveries.

title: Dog Dudes
subTitle: Dylan and Darien Northstar turn Red Zone dogs from dangerous to adorable while teaching their doggy parents along the way.

subTitle: An experimental drug exposes the evil within Dr. Jekyll (Anthony Andrews). With Laura Dern.

title: Dog Dudes
subTitle: Following Canine Rehabilitation Experts Dylan and Darien Northstar, and their amazing pack of Siberian Huskies. Together, they use ancient techniques only they have mastered to help troubled dogs become good dogs.

subTitle: Byron the cheetah visits the class.

subTitle: A ranger searches for a missing secret agent lioness.

subTitle: Lynne the giraffe is becoming tired of the limelight.

subTitle: A dog named Purdy needs two rear leg prosthetics; a dog named Honey has two broken wrists.

subTitle: A golden retriever named Purdy is in need of two rear leg prosthetics; Ibex the goat may be a good candidate for a brace.

subTitle: A dog named Huck needs a prosthetic; a doberman pincher named Fudge needs a brace.

title: Second Chance Pets
subTitle: A lovable pup named Cleo needs a brace to fix an injured back leg; a cadaver dog needs a brace to keep working.

title: Killer Instincts
subTitle: Takes a look at the animal kingdom's most opportunistic eaters and the set of skills they utilize to hunt their prey.

title: Killer Instincts
subTitle: Showcases the smallest yet most dangerous assassins in the animal kingdom and examines how they survive and hunt in the wild.

title: Killer Instincts
subTitle: From pumas to bald eagles to grizzly bears, witness apex predators using speed, strength and cunning to dominate their realms.

title: The Blue Realm
subTitle: Sea snakes; a small cone snail hunts, paralyses and eats its prey; therapeutic medicines can be made from the venom of fish and invertebrates.

title: The Blue Realm
subTitle: Whale sharks can reach nearly 50 feet in length and weigh 30 tons or more; the capture and transport of whale sharks to the Georgia Aquarium in Atlanta.

subTitle: Bear the Labrador has come in to see Stacey again for a sore knee; Stacey travels up to Auckland to assist Mark with a calf that's stuck in the birth canal.

title: INWILD
subTitle: All fish have moveable jaws and are covered in scales, using gills to breath underwater. Many forming schools or shoals based on social structure, it's a unique group arrangement with many benefits.

title: INWILD
subTitle: Invertebrates include all forms of insects, spiders, scorpions, centipedes and crustaceans, with nearly 10 million species; take a close look at the survival structure of bees, ants and spiders.

title: INWILD
subTitle: Our closest relatives, primates are a diverse group of highly intelligent mammals; over 440 species, with varied social structures and behaviors, explore chimpanzees, gorillas, orangutans and more.

title: INWILD
subTitle: From the deserts to the densest jungles, one group of mammals can be found unlike all the rest; a few are predatory but many carnivorous, marsupials range from the red kangaroo to the tiny mouse.

subTitle: A look at the crocodile and its ancestors that roamed Earth millions of years ago; how snakes rely on the heat of the sun to regulate their body temperature.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: A look at the unique ways in which different animals avoid danger and protect themselves, from the viselike jaws of the hyena to the gunshot speed of the frog.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: A look at how sea creatures have adapted methods of survival over time, from the deadly spines of a lionfish to the lightning speed of a barracuda.

title: Race of Life
subTitle: Examines how creatures protect themselves underwater and utilize an array of strategies just to stay alive.

subTitle: The king of the jungle is on the brink of being a thing of the past, but for now it remains one of the last wild predators on earth.

title: Wild About
subTitle: Based on superstition and misconception, only intensive preservation efforts and increased public awareness may save them, but even then, some say it may be too late; until that day Sharks will continue to stalk waters everywhere.

subTitle: A dog and a goat meet in their front-legged carts; an elderly Labradoodle hopes to stay active with a new knee brace; a German Shepherd-Mastiff mix needs an indoor slipper.

subTitle: Janice is being kept busy with a rare case of a dog who uses prosthetics on both front legs.

subTitle: Meat preparation gets weird for some as volunteers make dinner time more interesting; a brave zebra stallion faces the confident lions; Wakanaka proves her predatory prowess.
